Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

JCS 19, 121 [Royal inscription of Simbar-šipak][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

a-na dEN.LIL₂ EN šur-bi-i ša₂-qi₂-i LUGAL da-ri

(o 1) To Ellil, the supreme (and) elevated lord, the eternal king, the exalted leader, the lord of heaven and earth, the one who determines the fates, the very proud nobleman, whose rule cannot be rivalled during the era of the gods, the king of the gods, the lord of the extent of the heavens, to whose utterance all the Igigi cry out fearfully. And, at the giving of his orders, the Anunnaki, all of them, tremble there, the lord of the Ekur and Nippur, inhabitant of Ekur-igigal, his lord.

o 22

mas-su₃-u₂ ṣi-i-ri e-tel ANe u KIti₃ mu-šim NAM-MEŠ

o 33

kab-ti šit-ra-ḫu ša₂ i-na ⸢da⸣-ri DINGIR-MEŠ la iš-ša₂-an-na-nu EN-us-su

o 44

LUGAL DINGIR-MEŠ EN se-ḫi-ip ⸢ša₂⸣-ma-mi ša₂ a-na ṣi-it KA-šu₂ di₂-gi₃-gi₃ kul-lat-su-nu i-nu-qu pal-ḫiš

o 55

u₃ a-na na-da-nu ur-ti-šu₂ dA.NUN.NA.KI kiš-šat-su-nu i-šu-bu a-⸢pi⸣-šam

o 66

EN E₂.KUR u NIBRUki a-šib E₂.KUR.IGI.GAL₂ EN-šu₂

o 77

msim-bar—dši-i-pak LUGAL mi-ša₂-ru re-e-šu₂ ⸢šaḫ⸣-ṭi

(o 7) Simbar-šipak, the king of justice, the humble servant, the one who puts the ways of life of Anu and Dagan in order, the one who looks after their cult well.

o 88

muš-te-šir₃ al-ka-ka-a-ti da-nu₃ u₃ dda-gan mu-šal-li-mu me-si-šu₂-un

o 99

gišGU.ZA dEN.LIL₂ ša₂ E₂.KUR.IGI.⸢GAL₂⸣ ša mdNA₃—NIG₂.DU—URI₃ LUGAL maḫ-ri i-pu-uš

(o 9) The throne of Ellil of the Ekur-igigal, which Nebuchadnezzar, a former king, made.

o 1010

i-na BALe mdIŠKUR—DUMU.UŠ—MU LUGAL TIN.TIRki lu₂KUR₂ a-ra-mu u₃ su-tu-u₂

(o 10) In the reign of Adad-apla-iddina, the king of Babylon, Aramaean and Sutean enemies, enemies of the Ekur and Nippur, who desecrated Duranki, (and) who caused Sippar, the primordial city, seat of the chief justice of the gods to be robbed of their cults, carried off the land of the Sumerians and Akkadians, (and) made all the temples collapse.

o 1111

a-a-bi E₂.KUR u₃ NIBRUki mu-šal-pit₂ DUR.AN.KI

o 1212

ša₂ ZIMBIRki IRI ṣa-a-ti šu-bat DI.KU₅.GALla DINGIR-MEŠ u₂-šaḫ-bit me-si-šu₂-un

o 1313

iš-lul-u₂-ma KUR šu-me-ri u ak-ka-di-i u₂-šam-qi₂-tu ⸢gi⸣-mir E₂.KUR-MEŠ

o 1414

bu-ša₂-a NIG₂.GA dEN.LIL₂ ša₂ a-ra-mu it-ba-lu-ma i-ki-⸢mu⸣ su-bar-ti

(o 14) The goods and property of Ellil, which the Aramaeans took away and Subartu accumulated -

o 1515

ina qi₂-bit dEN.LIL₂ ma-lik DINGIR-MEŠ ša₂-qi-i ⸢EN⸣ EN.EN

(o 15) at the command of Ellil, the elevated prince of the gods, the lord of lords, (and of) Marduk, the eternal king, who has the power to abandon and resettle, (and of) Utulu, the terror of (his) enemies, the one who dissolves his opponents -

o 1616

dAMAR.UTU LUGAL da-ri ša₂ šu-ud-du-u₂ u₃ šu-šu-⸢bi₃⸣ ba-šu-u₂ KI-šu₂

o 1717

dUT.U₁₈.LU ḫur-ba-šu₂ a-a-bi ⸢muš₂-ḫar-mi⸣-ṭi ga-re-[e]

o 1818

bu-ša₂-a NIG₂.GA dEN.LIL₂ aš+šurki-u₂ i-du-u₂-ma u₂-še-ri-ib qe₂-reb BAL.TIL[ki]

(o 18) the Assyrians knowingly brought the goods and property of Ellil into the centre of Baltil.

Reverse
r 1r 1

ul-tu qe₂-reb BAL.TILki a-na DUR.AN.KI i-tu-ru aš₂-ru-uš-⸢šu₂⸣

(r 1) From the centre of Baltil, they returned (it) to Duranki, its place.

r 22

msim-bar—dši-i-pak GIR₃.NITA₂ d⸢EN⸣.LIL₂ x x na-ram d[...]

(r 2) Simbar-šipak, the governor of Ellil, ..., the loved one of [DN], the reverent shepherd who knows how to be assiduous towards Ellil's shrines, became exceedingly concerned about the renewal of the Ekur and Nippur.

r 33

re-ʾu pal-ḫu ša₂ aš₂-rat dEN.LIL₂ ši-te-⸢ʾa⸣-a mu-du-[u₂]

r 44

a-na ud-du-uš E₂.KUR u₃ NIBRUki ik-kud it-⸢ta⸣-ʾi-id [...]

r 55

gišGU.ZA gišMES GIŠ da-ru-u₂ ša₂ iḫ-zi-šu₂ ṣa-ri-ri-um-ma šu-tu-ru x-uš-[...]

(r 5) As a symbol of the exalted power of Ellil, he had made and set up a throne of mesu-wood, lasting wood, the settings of which were of red gold and the ... [...] were very great.

r 66

a-na si-ma-a-ti dEN.LIL₂u₂-ti-šu₂ ṣir-ti u₂-še-piš-ma iš-[kun]

r 77

a-na šat-ti dAMAR.UTU EN GALu₂ dEN.LIL₂ DINGIR-MEŠ [ḫa-diš] ⸢lip⸣-[pa-lis]

(r 7) Thereupon, may Marduk, the great lord, the Ellil of the gods, [joyfully] look upon (him). s

r 88

i-na gišGU.ZA šu-a-šu₂ ina a-ša₂-bi-šu₂ ši-mat m⸢sim⸣-[bar]—⸢dši⸣-[i-pak]

(r 8) When settling him on the throne, may he (Marduk) determine the fate of Simbar-šipak, the king of justice, the shepherd, his favourite, to good fortune.

r 99

LUGAL mi-ša₂-ru re-ʾi bi-bil lib₃-bi-šu₂ ana MUNUS.⸢SIG₅ li⸣-iš-⸢ša-kin⸣

(colophon)
r 1010

ki-ma SUMUN-šu₂ ša₂-⸢ṭir⸣-ma IGI.TAB ŠU-MIN mdAMAR.UTU—LUGAL-a-ni A-šu₂ ša₂ mri-mu-ut—dNA₃

(r 10) Written and checked according to its original. Hand of Marduk-šarrani, son of Rimut-Nabu, son of Luṣi-ana-nur-Erra, descendant of Sin-leqi-unninni, the lamentation priest of Ištar of Uruk and Nanaya, temple-enterer of Kanisura, [scribe] of Eana.

r 1111

A m luE₃—a-na—ZALAG₂—dER₃.RA ŠA₃.BAL.⸢BAL⸣ mdSUEN—TI—⸢A.RA⸣.ZU lu₂GALA

r 1212

dINANA UNUGki u dna-na-a lu₂KU₄.E₂ d⸢KA⸣.NI.SUR.⸢RA⸣ [lu₂DUB.SAR]⸢ri E₂⸣.AN.NA

r 1313

ṭup-pi mri-mu-ut—dNA₃ ŠA₃.BAL.⸢BAL⸣ mdSUEN—TI—A.RA.⸢ZU⸣1

(r 13) Tablet of Rimut-Nabu, descendant of Sin-leqi-unninni, the lamentation priest of Ištar of Uruk and Nanaya, temple-enterer of Kanisura.

r 1414

lu₂GALA dINANA UNUGki u dna-na-a lu₂KU₄.E₂ dKA.NI.SUR.RA2

r 1515

itiGAN.GAN.NA U₄ 1-KAM₂ ša₂-ṭir

(r 15) Written on the first day of Kislimu (month IX).

1ṭup-pi {m}(erasure)ri-mu-ut--{d}NA₃. ligature d+NA₃

2There is an erasure at the end of this line. ligature d+INANA
